title: hopea longirostrata
intro: Hopea longirostrata, also known as the long-beaked Hopea, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Hopea longirostrata lie in its bark, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. Hopea longirostrata is often used in herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, which can be consumed as teas or used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
